stories.headline: St Patrick's Festival Serves Celtic Feasts at Pike Place
stories.body: Kells Irish Restaurant transforms into a 12-day Celtic celebration at Pike Place Market with traditional fare, live folk ballads, and rugby watch parties. Daily performances run through March 17th. Meanwhile, Brazilian chef Emme Collins is set to debut Baiana at Pike Place in early March, serving acarajé and moqueca from her competition-winning kitchen.

stories.headline: High School Jazz Bands Take Center Stage Downtown
stories.body: Top jazz bands from five local high schools perform at Hot Java Cool Jazz in The Paramount Theatre. See Her Be Her returns for International Women's Day with panels on women in electronic music, plus a nighttime dance party headlined by BEXXIE.

stories.headline: Spring Adventure Fair Welcomes Hiking Season
stories.body: Spring Into Adventure kicks off outdoor season at Lake Sammamish State Park on March 28th. The Snohomish Wine Festival at Thomas Family Farm offers tastings and vineyard tours on March 7th. Perfect timing as Seattle shakes off winter hibernation.

stories.headline: Comic Con Costume Parade Invades Convention Center
stories.body: Emerald City Comic Con encourages attendees to wear costumes for this giant comics, anime, and pop culture event at Seattle Convention Center. Even non-attendees enjoy watching the colorful costumed fans walking downtown sidewalks.

stories.headline: Secret Burger Kitchen Sneaks Into Kirkland
stories.body: Originally hidden inside a Tacoma hotel, Secret Burger Kitchen opens its second location in Kirkland's Juanita Village this March. The smashburger specialist brings their covert culinary operations across the lake.
